title: Heiner Marxen
text: Home * People * Heiner Marxen Heiner Marxen at BELCT 2001 [1] Heiner Marxen , a German computer scientist and software engineer, M.Sc. in computer science from Technical University of Berlin in 1986, and author of Chest , a Program for Solving Orthodox Chess Problems [2] . Heiner is a well known Busy Beaver expert [3] . Additionally he introduced a method to compute Rook Paths Puzzles [4] [5] . In 1973, he has written the first precursor of Chest in Fortran-II (no recursion ) to solve mate-in-two problems [6] . It did not need or use any tapes and ran on an IBM-1130 with 32 K bytes, and took half an hour for one job [7] .
